content:
# Well Completion

**Summary**: The process of making a well ready for production or injection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017]] evaluated fluids specifically designed for the completion and workover of HPHT wells.

- [[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]] addressed the challenges of well completion in HPHT environments.

- [[1997-al-gahtani|Al-Gahtani, 1997]] studied the impact of completion scenarios on overall performance.

## Scientific Context

- [[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017]] Focuses on ensuring safety and productivity in high-pressure environments.

- [[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]] addressed the challenges of well completion in HPHT environments.

- [[1997-al-gahtani|Al-Gahtani, 1997]] studied the impact of completion scenarios on overall performance.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017]] Fluid design and laboratory testing.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Determined that micronized barite is an effective weighting agent to mitigate issues with traditional solids. ([[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017, p. 13]])


- Not stated in source ([[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]])

- Perforation distribution significantly affects the flow profile; even 20% perforation at the heel can yield double the production of the same amount at the toe. ([[1997-al-gahtani|Al-Gahtani, 1997, p. 41]])
